title: Judge: cross-check verdict vs checks + NEEDS_REVISION reopens a capped cell
action: skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/judge.mjs validateJudgeVerdict (line 37) validates schema+each check but NEVER cross-checks verdict against check statuses -> accepts verdict:PASS with a FAIL check, and NEEDS_REVISION with all checks PASS (confirmed judge.mjs:75-83). Add: reject when verdict===PASS and any check.status===FAIL (a PASS with a failing check is an invalid verdict); reject when verdict===NEEDS_REVISION and no check.status===FAIL (revision demanded with nothing failing). Keep the existing failure_signature-required rule. SEPARATELY close the post-cap hole: recordJudgeVerdict (cells.mjs:1778) records a verdict without touching cell.status, so a NEEDS_REVISION recorded AFTER a worker already capped is toothless (re-cap says already-capped; capCell guard at 1143-1151 only fires on cap attempts). Make recordJudgeVerdict, when it appends a NEEDS_REVISION verdict to a cell whose status===capped, transition that cell back to claimed (rework_required) under withStoreLock, logging the reopen, so the verdict has teeth. Do NOT add new cell.status enum values (too high-blast for RC). Mirror judge.mjs + cells.mjs to .bee/bin/lib byte-identical; regenerate plugin trees + manifest.

truths: validateJudgeVerdict rejects verdict:PASS with any FAIL check, and NEEDS_REVISION with zero FAIL checks; a consistent verdict still validates and failure_signature stays required when any check is FAIL.
recordJudgeVerdict appending NEEDS_REVISION to a capped cell reopens it to claimed (rework) under withStoreLock, logged; recording on a non-capped cell is unchanged.
No new cell.status enum value added; existing capCell NEEDS_REVISION guard + --override-judge audit path unchanged.
judge.mjs and cells.mjs mirrored byte-identical (.bee/bin/lib); full run_verify.mjs green; a regression net proves the two new cross-check rejections RED-first then GREEN.
prohibitions: Do NOT add judge_pending/rework_required to CELL_STATUSES.
Do NOT weaken existing judge/cap guards.

trace.outcome: judge.mjs validateJudgeVerdict now cross-checks verdict against check statuses (PASS+FAIL-check and NEEDS_REVISION+no-FAIL-check both rejected); cells.mjs recordJudgeVerdict reopens a capped cell to claimed when a NEEDS_REVISION verdict lands on it, logged as trace.reopened_for_rework + a decision. recordJudgeVerdict is now withStoreLock-wrapped (async) to make the status flip a safe read-check-write; the sole CLI caller (bee.mjs handleCellsJudgeRecord, both copies) and all test_lib.mjs call sites were updated to await it. RED-before/GREEN-after regression net added and verified by temporarily reverting the implementation (422 passed incl. 2 new tests; reverting drops to 420 passed/2 failed on exactly the new tests).
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": ["skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs (judge-verdict/1 + recordJudgeVerdict sections)"],
  "tests_added_or_changed": [
    "validateJudgeVerdict rejects an inconsistent PASS (a FAIL check present) and an inconsistent NEEDS_REVISION (no FAIL check present); a consistent verdict of either kind still validates (new)",
    "recordJudgeVerdict: a NEEDS_REVISION verdict recorded against a capped cell reopens it to claimed (rework), logged in trace.reopened_for_rework; a NEEDS_REVISION on an open/claimed cell leaves status untouched; a PASS on a capped cell leaves it capped (new)",
    "the two pre-existing recordJudgeVerdict checks updated to await the now-async function and use assertRejects instead of assertThrows for its refusals"
  ],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Before this cell: judge.mjs:37 validateJudgeVerdict validated schema + each check's {id,status,evidence} but never cross-checked the top-level verdict against check statuses, so {verdict:PASS, checks:[{status:FAIL,...}], failure_signature:...} and {verdict:NEEDS_REVISION, checks:[{status:PASS,...}]} both validated ok:true. Separately cells.mjs:1778 recordJudgeVerdict appended a NEEDS_REVISION verdict to trace.semantic_judge but never touched cell.status, so a NEEDS_REVISION recorded AFTER a worker already capped left the cell status:'capped' forever (capCell's own NEEDS_REVISION guard at line ~1145 only fires on a cap attempt, never re-fires post-cap). Confirmed by stashing the implementation files back to HEAD and re-running test_lib.mjs: the two new tests FAILED (420 passed, 2 failed) against the unfixed code before this cell's changes; popping the stash and re-running gave 422 passed, 0 failed.",
  "verification_run": "node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS run_verify: 35 suite(s) (exit 0), run twice; one interleaved run hit the known intermittent scripts/test_store_lock.mjs timing flake (a single named-suite FAIL with a stale-takeover mutual-exclusion count, unrelated to judge.mjs/cells.mjs), and a clean re-run came back green (exit 0)",
  "deliberate_exceptions": []
}
